title: Xi Jinping's 29-Nation Open AI Bloc vs Washington's Closed-Model Gatekeeper - TCR 07/19/26
description:
Xi Jinping gathered 29 nations at Shanghai's World AI Conference around freely downloadable Chinese open-weight models, Treasury Secretary Bessent's plan for an independent SEC-reporting AI certifier landed under White House review as Hassabis, Altman, and Amodei each backed independent testing, while Hassabis proposed a single US-led testing body, and enterprise DRAM lead times stretched past 40 weeks as SK Group's chairman called doubling memory demand a national security issue.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-july-19-2026/

0:00 Open and framing
0:48 Xi makes open weights a new world order
4:59 Washington moves toward a FINRA-for-AI
9:18 The memory crunch reaches the server rack
12:41 Data-center boom triggers largest US utility merger
16:36 Speech implant skips personal calibration
19:30 Close

This episode covers China's 29-nation AI cooperation organization, Washington's FINRA-for-AI certifier proposal, the memory crunch reaching the server rack, the $67 billion NextEra-Dominion utility merger driven by data-center demand, and a Duke speech implant that skips months of per-patient calibration.

The day's signal is two capitals building opposite containers for the same thing while the capability keeps equalizing regardless. In Shanghai, Xi framed gated frontier access as a "historical injustice," and China launched an AI cooperation organization backed by 29 nations; the Nasdaq slipped as chip names priced for permanent scarcity re-rated. In Washington, a Treasury plan for a self-funded body that certifies who may ship a frontier model arrived as all three Western lab heads published memos backing independent testing, while Hassabis proposed the single-body design - a gate whose high fixed compliance costs double as a wall against the next entrant. Underneath both, memory lead times past 40 weeks and a $67 billion NextEra-Dominion merger show the physical bottleneck neither doctrine can wish away, forcing the cost of compute onto the books that created it. And a Duke team pooled neural recordings across patients into a shared latent space, beating patient-specific models and dissolving the calibration burden that kept restored speech scarce.

- Tech Times - Inventec Warns AI Memory Crunch Has Reached Servers: https://www.techtimes.com/articles/320681/20260716/inventec-warns-ai-memory-crunch-has-reached-servers-lead-times-hit-40-plus-weeks.htm
- Nature Communications - Cross-Patient Neural Alignment for Speech Decoding: https://www.nature.com/articles/s41467-026-75455-1
- Shared Sapience - The Last Difficult Decade: 2025-2035: https://sharedsapience.com/the-last-difficult-decade-2025-2035/
